2201588:
Zandrek's Pesh Palace

Zandrek's Pesh Palace is a famous pesh parlor in the city of Katapesh. It is a round, domed building surrounded by bronze columns. Its massive copper door is a sculpted face of a laughing man. There are no other doors in the interior but only silk and bead curtains. Multiple private chambers line the first ring of the building while a second ring is filled with statuary, cushions, couches, and wandering minstrels and dancing girls. The center of the structure is a lovely courthouse filled with plants, marble flagstones and a central pool full of colorful fish. Zandrek's Pesh Palace is the most popular den of iniquity in a city full of sin and vice. Master Zandrek cares to satisfy all of his customers' desires. A small army of exotic slaves are especially trained to see to the needs of Master Zandrek's guests.

8033048:
Diggen's Rest

Diggen's Rest is a watchtower in the Verduran Forest of eastern Andoran, north of the town of Fusil and on the border between Andoran and Galt. It served as the final location of the records from Pathfinder lodges once founded by Kerinha Napsunar, one of the first Pathfinders.
9096848:
Seraph's Ladder

Seraph's Ladder is a freestanding golden and white marble ladder that stands on the Blood Plains of the Hold of Belkzen. It is believed that Seraph's Ladder is one of the few remnants of the Thassilonian domain of Gastash, dating from the reign of Zutha, Runelord of Gluttony.
9646798:
Wreck of the Impdrake

The Wreck of the Impdrake is located near the corals of Lady's Cape, having been the site where a crew of Chelish merchants wrecked their ship on the coast when their captain, Linus Halfclasper, mistook the Lady's Light for one of the lighthouses of Magnimar. Those who managed to swim ashore were killed by the boggards of the region over the next two days.
9680008:
Taurean Embassy

The Taurean Embassy is a residence built in the style of Iblydos, and it's where one of Absalom's most curious inhabitants, the albino minotaur Nuar Spiritskin, lives. He was banished by his tribe from the Kortos Mounts, and being just over seven feet tall, he had no trouble integrating into human society. When he intervened to save a man being beaten by sailors, his life changed drastically, for this man was none other than Lord Gyr on a confidential mission. Gyr bestowed upon him the title of Prince of Minotaurs, but he knew it was merely a flight of fancy from the Primarch. Now, he works to keep the streets crime-free, with no aspirations to leadership, content simply to have a place to call home.
21802548:
Palace of the Brumal Lords

The frozen Palace of the Brumal Lords is where the three Brumal Lords are magically sealed, as they have been for the past 1,400 years. The three fey Baeloc, Nojec, and Yahlo were once allied with Baba Yaga, but after the Holvirgang Uprising they retreated here and refused any attempts at peace. So Queen Jadwiga used the Lock of Pajari to seal them within its walls.

Gremlins are the only creatures that can still get in and out of the palace, and will sometimes raid nearby villages.
21835138:
Isle of Arenway

The Isle of Arenway is located at the confluence of the Verduran Fork and Sellen River in central Taldor. Set in the heart of the Verduran Forest, it is the headquarters of the local druids of the Wildwood Lodge. It is said to contain a series of standing stones which create an annual connection to the demiplane known as the Circle Between every solstice.

In their summer solstice Moot of Ages, the Green Faith druids from all over Golarion gather in the Isle of Arenway to bring their discoveries before the entire druid community.

The Isle also contains an unnamed harbour outpost of the Taldan River Guard, although by the Treaty of the Wildwood, its sailors are forbidden to leave their walled compound, except to patrol the Sellen River and Verduran Fork. Ships traveling past often stop at the outpost to have their vessels blessed by the druids.
24152878:
Xin-Edasseril

Xin-Edasseril was the capital of the ancient Thassilonian realm of envy, Edasseril. It is now the capital of the kingdom of Edasseril within the nation of New Thassilon.

57863078:
Usaro

The majority charau-ka city of Usaro is the former domain of the Gorilla King who served Angazhan, demon lord of beasts. The city is located on the Blood River where it flows into Lake Ocota from its southern shoreline. Its inhabitants are feared throughout the Mwangi Expanse, and the former king's treasure vaults are reputed to contain priceless riches, including the mighty bombard Worldbreaker, but the ominous sound of human-skin drums carrying across the lake deters all but the bravest or most foolhardy of treasure hunters.
99146808:
Snowford

Snowford is a village nestled in the foothills of the Mindspin Mountains of eastern Nidal close to the border with Molthune. It is dedicated to goat herding and famous for producing Snowserpent's Kiss, a local alcoholic beverage. Every year during the winter solstice, an event called the Glassmoon Witch occurs, where a circle of obsidian stones five miles from the village is illuminated and an ancient shrine appears. It is said that those who offer gifts throughout the year can ask the spirit for a blessing, but only if the request is altruistic and helps others.

The inhabitants of Snowford avoid revealing the location or effects of the Glassmoon Witch, fearing the wrath of the Umbral Court, which would consider it heretical, especially as some associate the phenomenon with Shelyn. The village mayor, Wistea Glasswing, keeps the village's attention at bay by ensuring that visitors leave early and never discover the village's secret.
105668298:
Fangspire

Fangspire is one of the shelters of the Abendego Piercing created by the Knights of Salvation after the Eye of Abendego devastated the Sodden Lands.

The magically constructed tower of stone and metal has withstood the storms for centuries and remains in good condition. However, the storms have undermined its foundations, and as a result the tower leans over the sea at a dangerous angle.

Fangspire is the traditional domain of the largest of the region's boggard tribes. The constant warfare between the various boggard tribes ensures no tribe can claim the honour for very long.
108332448:
Cloven Hoof Society

The Cloven Hoof Society is an organisation that helps cambions throughout the city of Kintargo, the nation of Ravounel, and lands beyond. Its headquarters are located in the slums of the city's Redroof neighborhood.
110044058:
Heslandaena

Heslandaena is a subterranean settlement below the island of Dahak's Tooth in the Shackles. The cenotes that dot the island offer aquatic entry to miles of sea caves, which eventually emerge into unflooded, humid caverns. These moldy dark caves and passages lead to Heslandaena. The isolated town is inhabited by drow cut off from the Darklands many years ago by a curse.

The dark elves were part of the Harkule clan. They offended a powerful matriarch and were banished for their insolence. The isolated drow town is ruled by matriarch Pelanquelle, wicked and resentful for her exile. A number of pirate captains know about the drow settlement and are trusted by its ruler: Adis Vraisdottir, captain of the fast sloop North Wind, which sails out of Mezdrubal; White Hollis Dobilo, captain of the brig Stormrunner, which sails from Drenchport; and Constanza Purgote, captain of the sloop Constanza's Whim which sails from Port Peril.

The three pirates, each unaware of the others, all have lucrative trading deals with matriarch Pelanquelle bringing foodstuffs and supplies in exchange for raw gemstones and rare subterranean substances.
123211968:
Drezen

Founded as a city-fortress during the hubris of the First Mendevian Crusade, Drezen was overtaken by the demonic hordes of the Worldwound. For years, it stood as a demonic monument to the foolish naivety of those early crusaders, until it was freed with the rest of the Worldwound by members of the Fifth Mendevian Crusade in 4718 AR.
132106938:
Shoreline

3 locations:
  • Groggy Froggy
  • Statue Street
  • Tomb of the Living
135704298:
Satyr Shoals

The Satyr Shoals are a group of islands in the southern part of Iblydos formed by sand and shells being carried up from the Obari Ocean. The unstable nature of the islands means permanent settlements are frequently buried or resurfaced as the topology of the islands change. Because of this, none of the nearby city-states want the land, and anyone found here are likely pirates or other types who want to stay away from the eyes of any authorities.
139654768:
Broken Bridge

Broken Bridge is a massive ruined causeway that once crossed its namesake Brokenbridge River. Located halfway between the city of Yanmass and the start of the Verduran Fork, it was destroyed by a silver dragon sometime during the Dragon Plague between 3660 and 3672 AR. Rather than rebuild the bridge, locals left it as a memorial to the destruction and horrors of dragonkind's decade-long rampage across the country. Instead, the locals built a smaller wooden bridge next to the original Broken Bridge, along with a small stone fort made from some of the remnants of the destroyed bridge. The fort now houses members of the River Guard.

All that remains of the original Broken Bridge are the pilings and a few spans. Local youths sometimes dare each other to try to cross the bridge by leaping between these, a feat seemingly physically impossible and not achieved within living memory.
143900648:
Geb's Rest

The small settlement of Geb's Rest, sardonically called Bitter End by the locals, is located on the northern border of the nation of Geb, on the edge of the Spellscar Desert. It is little more than a ruin, but this dusty hamlet serves as a supply post on the road which connects the city of Graydirge to the south, through the Mana Wastes and all the way to the city of Ecanus in Nex.
158159658:
Erithiel's Hall

Erithiel's Hall is a serpentine earthen mound located in the western Fierani Forest of Kyonin. Several stories in height, between 20 feet to 100 feet across and several miles in length, the mound is said to predate the elves and is rumoured to be connected to the fey of the First World. According to the most popular myth, Erithiel's Hall is named for an ancient fey hero, Erithiel, who in the dawn of history fought against the earth serpent Garukresh. Their epic battle is said to have left deserts and seas in its wake before Garukresh was finally slain where he now lies at Erithiel's Hall. The victorious Erithiel supposedly then built a vast barrow-palace within the remains of his defeated foe. Those few elves who have tried to excavate Erithiel's Hall have found themselves plagued with bad luck and earning the hatred of the region's fey creatures.
159567088:
Akafuto

Akafuto, Minkai's second-largest port, is located at the mouth of the Tagiryu River and serves as a major part of the Tian Xia lumber trade. Akafuto exports a wide variety of raw and finished goods, and the city's junks are among the most common Minkaian ships in foreign ports, particularly Tianjing.

Akafuto's most famous temple is the Ukuashi-Ji, dedicated to the god of prosperity Kofusachi, who is said to bless the harvested goods and crafts that flow through Akafuto's markets. Akafuto is also home to the Juhimeiyo School, a monastic institution attended primarily by young nobles looking to work within the imperial court.

Akafuto has maintained a remarkable political stability over the centuries. It has been ruled by a member of the Moniwa family for over a millennia.

Irorium

177776298:
Garizés City

Sitting in the north-western reaches of the Arcadian nation of Xopatl, Garizés City is renowned for the prominent musical style that is named after it. This musical style is a lively one typified by an inclusion of common instruments, like flutes, guitars and trumpets, alongside the more unusual garizan, a musical instrument made of entwined plant matter that is squeezed and stretched to make different sounds. Garizés City is the primary place that garizans are manufactured and those that are not made in Garizés City are normally viewed as being of inherently inferior quality.
183430678:
Grave of Moyoshi Shoda

The grave of Moyoshi Shoda is the burial site of the hobgoblin warlord who founded the nation of Kaoling. The site includes a shrine of four statues depicting Shoda as a youth, a young leader, Kaoling's Warlord, and an elder advisor, and the crypt containing the bones of Shoda and his War Generals of the Council of Nine. The crypt is also surrounded by grave markers of those killed during the War of Redemption.

Military leaders make pilgrimages to the site, which is tended to and protected by an honor guard.

223246798:
Orlov

Orlov, the former coastal capital and oldest city of Iobaria, is today but a ruin of its former glory. Located on the Lake of Mists and Veils, Orlov was founded in 753 AR by explorers from Okormirr who discovered the ancient Koloran Roads. Built on top of the ruins of a cyclopean city, Orlov grew quickly, becoming larger than Orkomirr in 764 AR, and eventually the first proper city in the nation of Njalgard.

In 905 AR, Iobar, heir of Orlov's ruler Kjell, usurped the nine koffars of Njalgard and established the nation of Iobaria, with Orlov as its capital. However, after the fall of Old Iobaria in 3150 AR, Orlov was conquered by foreign powers and their pawns. In 3212 AR, Orlov was reclaimed by three warlords, marking the start of the century-long War of Restoration. During the period of New Iobaria that followed, Antoll served as capital instead of Orlov.

During 4000 AR the city was attacked by, and repelled, an invasion by frost giants from the Ice Steppes who had pillaged their way across the nation. In 4659 AR, the "plague of boils and bone-aches" hit Franax, and that town's great shipworks moved to Orlov.

Due to the town's numerous ups and downs, its population now numbers at less than half of its former numbers, and much of the city has crumbled into ruins, with the stonemasons walling off complexes deemed too ruined to inhabit. Its scheming prince, Tzakiv Korya, dreams of re-establishing the Empire, but he lacks enough troops to fully man the city's ancient fortifications. Still, the Prince has managed to build some alliances with adventure-seekers and investors in Cheliax and the River Kingdoms.

The city contains many towering structures, built by cyclopes.
223280568:
Church of Asmodeus

The Church of Asmodeus is one of the youngest building of the Ascendant Court, the religious heart of Absalom, with the previous high temple to Asmodeus having been burned to the ground during the so-called "Devil's Night Uprising" in 4698 AR. Instead of rebuilding on the old grounds, the new Church of Asmodeus was built, it was completed in 4705 AR, just months before the centenary anniversary of the death of Aroden. While Infernal Cheliax is the home of Asmodeus's worship in the Inner Sea region, the Church of Asmodeus in Absalom serves as a home for those outside of Cheliax. Worshippers at the Church of Asmodeus include ancestries that normally face prejudice and persecution within Cheliax, such as tieflings, halflings and even the rare strix. This acceptance puts the Church somewhat at odds with Cheliax and the nearby Chelaxian embassy. In particular, the high priestess of the Church of Asmodeus, the so-called "Beloved of Asmodeus", Scion Lady Xansippe of House Tevineg is caught between her unwavering devotion to the faith of Asmodeus and her duty to both her ancestral homeland of Cheliax and her husband Lord Gulv of House Tevineg, the recently appointed grand ambassador of Cheliax.
232764018:
Sipplerose

The small gnome community of Sipplerose, located in the northern Sanos Forest of Varisia, is the home of the lyrakien Novi.
265976908:
Rickety bridge

An old rickety bridge spans the Thorn River about a dozen miles south of the Thorn River bandit camp. The river is about 20-foot-deep here, passing between two rugged hills. Local bandits are aware that the creaking bridge will surely collapse if they do not cross it one by one.
295412008:
Tiven's Reed

Tiven's Reed is a small gnome settlement in central Katapesh, very similar to Yavipho. It is located near an oasis and began as a stopover for merchant caravans.
301441218:
Stavian Ranches

The Stavian Ranches are home to the finest horses in all of Taldor, for they are the horses ridden by the royal lineage. Founded by Stavian I, the stables breed Taldan horses used by the cavalry, although breeding Qadiran horses is forbidden by royal decree. Stavian III's horse, Dycephalus, traces his lineage back to the horse ridden by his great-grandfather, Kaillerophon.
316910118:
Humbolt's Outfitters

Humbolt's Outfitters is a general store run by the merchant Gron Humbolt in The Coins and thanks to his wife, Lady Myleena, it has gained a great reputation in recent years. It occupies two floors in a stone structure that could easily be mistaken for a fortress, were it not for the arches, crates, and shelves inside, overflowing with all sorts of items. From clothing and tools to rations, the store has absolutely everything one could possibly need, whether for adventures in the Immenwood or climbing in the Kortos Mounts; if it exists, it's here. By Gron's rule, no magical items are sold in the store, directing customers to the Arcanamirium or his friend Engleton Embrey if they require such things. His wife sometimes works, mostly behind the scenes, and thanks to her support, they have been able to open satellite stores in the Foreign Quarter and Westgate.

335962978:
Sepet Pass

Sepet Pass is a high pass in the Brazen Peaks in Osirion on the route between Ipeq and El-Fatar. At the top of the pass, the Hostel Sepet provides safety for travelers.
335981128:
Nargrym's Tomb

Nargrym's Tomb is the resting place of the legendary giantslayer Nargrym Steelhand. The tomb was built north of Janderhoff in a forest in the Mindspin Mountains and has been forgotten due to the treacherous actions of the giantslayer's companion, leading to the belief that it is haunted.
340132848:
Koeh Lau

Koeh Lau is a remote valley in the northern Wall of Heaven mountains of Tian Xia. It is thought to be a place of idyllic beauty and contentment, home to a people who live with each other in peace and harmony without the need for laws or government. The truth, however, is much darker, as Koeh Lau is the forbidden domain of a group of vampiric monks, witches, and oracles who oversee the human population like cattle. The vampires allow them freedom during the day so that they may feed on them to their heart's content at night.
342487198:
Badelund

Badelund is a well-fortified town on the Frozen Road, in the north of the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold.

Badelund is surrounded by high walls to protect the small peasant town from uninvited fey visitors. Feyfrost fey are notorious child-stealers. Badelund is the first village on the Frozen Road west of Isseld and hosts a number of inns and taverns to serve the tired merchants sailing the Frozen Road. Baroness Pavlina is the ruler of the town. Because of her kindness and her love for a peasant, Pavlina was sent to Badelund as a punishment. The Baroness and her Ulfen husband Oddvar Pavelsson are wise governors and do their best to make peasants' life bearable. It is well known that Pavlina sends mercenary groups north to retrieve stolen children. Such groups are the Home Again Safe led by Helena Oukarnion and the White Dawn Hunters led by Mikhail Shalanov.
393956168:
Orjotoro

Orjotoro is a city located in eastern Xopatl, where the Limardero River flows into the Follower's River. It is home to the merchant's guild that monitors imports and exports between Xopatl, Razatlan, and Innazpa via Follower's River, and sees constant traffic from boats picking up and delivering goods. It is the central city of the three Follower's Rests on the river that serve as trade checkpoints along its length.
400481308:
Blood Fountain

The Blood Fountain is a Ghol-Gan ruin on the island of Dahak's Tooth in the Shackles. A magical cistern stands at the center of the ancient ruined settlement. The cistern appears to be fully functional, save that red, bilious liquid constantly pours from it, seeping into the ground around it and ensuring that no flora grows there.
417791438:
Minderhal's Valley

Minderhal's Valley is located within the Mindspin Mountains, surrounded by high mountains in an area where a river flows. This valley contains a cathedral dedicated to Minderhal, the giant god. Various tribes of giants have inhabited the valley, considering it sacred ground and defending it against intruders.
419852118:
Yahuima's Spring

Yahuima's Spring is a geyser located in the east of Xopatl, near to the Mildanesi Mountains that form much of the nation's border. It is said that Yahuima's Spring was created when the hero-god Yahuima wept after her lover, a fellow hero-god, ascended to true divinity and her tears created this geyser. To this day, Yahuima's Spring is infused with divine energy and, whenever it erupts, its water causes nearby plants to bloom with miraculous speed. This miracle normally draws visitors when the geyser is most active during the winter season, however recently these eruptions have instead spread a wave of destruction, blighting plants and corrupting animals.
422694278:
Painted Flutes

The Painted Flutes is a vast canyon nestled within the Barrier Wall mountains of western Katapesh. The valley is named for the unique geological phenomena found within, including dozens of hoodoos—tall, wind carved pillars of rock that look like totem poles—that are made of a variety of layers of different, brightly-coloured rocks as a spectacular landscape. The wind howls through the valley and when it reaches the right speed in creates haunting music like some sort of giant wind instrument.

Due to the forces of erosion constantly changing its shape, as well as its strong winds, the direction and duration the song created by the hoodoos is never the same twice. The valley attracts many bards and other musicians to hear its wondrous song. The valley is also inhabited by clans of janns and wandering air elementals who roam the deep canyons.
428453518:
Golden Cathedral

3 locations:
  • Avenue of the Gods
  • Godsgate
  • Grand Exchange
450396198:
Pulgari Estate

The Pulgari Estate was built by Bridden Pulgari in the hopes of contacting Mammon through it, taking advantage of a portal to Hell beneath the estate. The ritual failed, and the portal is now permanently open, gaining the attention of infernal and celestial beings alike, such as Ambithas, a gate archon.
461764468:
Moonwing's Bough

Moonwing's Bough is a small pocket of oak trees in the evergreen Grungir Forest which hosts an ancient shrine to Desna built thousands of years ago, during the reign of the Thassilonian Empire. The shrine is one of the most sacred sites of Desna's faith but very few worshipers attempt to visit it, traveling through the dangerous Grungir Forest. Moonwing's Bough is named after the first priest of Desna who reached the shrine in the modern age. The shrine is preserved in very good condition.
464470928:
Xin-Shalast

The fabled city of Xin-Shalast was once the capital of the Thassilonian realm of greed, Shalast, under the reign of Runelord Karzoug. Located far from civilization in the heart of the Kodar Mountains, Xin-Shalast's existence was considered mythical until it was recently rediscovered, and the restored Runelord Sorshen selected it as the capital of her revived New Thassilonian nation of Eurythnia in 4718 AR.
466947928:
Indapatta

Indapatta is the breathtakingly beautiful capital of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra that was founded by the first Maharajah Khiben-Sald. The city is located within the Golden Basin region of Vudra on the north-eastern banks of Sona Lake.
480116858:
Mount of Black Fire

The Mount of Black Fire is an extraordinary mountain in the Tusk Mountains. Unlike other active volcanoes, this mountain spews forth a dark ebony flame. Even more strangely, this ebony fire puts out regular fire. This unstable fire comes from overlapping gates connected to the Plane of Fire and the Void inside the mountain.

Of particular interest is the attraction of the Blackfire Adepts, a group of powerful individuals associated with an arcane cult. The Blackfire Adepts are drawn to the Mount of Black Fire due to its connection to distant planes and the eldritch energies it possesses. However, despite their efforts, they have not yet discovered a means to fully master or control this unique and enigmatic power source.
495437608:
Hammer Rock

Located in the Gravelands, the fortress of Hammer Rock was damaged—but remarkably not destroyed—by Tar-Baphon's super-weapon known as the Radiant Fire in 4719 AR.
523113208:
Citadel Enferac

Citadel Enferac is the stronghold of the Hellknight Order of the Gate. Located in the Menador Mountains, Citadel Enferac is the primary place where Hellknight signifers (spellcasters) are trained.

Constructed by inhuman hands, only a small part of the fortress rises upon the slopes of Mount Nyisaid. The larger part of the citadel lies within the mountain, spread through great caverns and halls. The smoke of numerous forges and laboratories rises from the fortress like eerie pennants; and spiked towers and precarious walkways mar the higher slopes. At the top of the mountain, the iron statue of a huge Hellknight guards a gate for flying visitors. The citadel includes garrisons, training halls, armories, libraries, workshops, and fortifications. Tales also tell of a prison for terrible creatures within the citadel which resembles a pit into a black eternity.

Deep within Citadel Enferac is a portal that leads to the Razor Stair, a library in Stygia, fifth level of Hell. The Order of the Gate's signifers frequently go there to consult with Mahshoka and Sister Drove, two uniilas who curate the Razor Stair. Despite decades of control, the Order of the Gate has not explored far beyond the portal due to the difficulty of navigating the Razor Stair; nevertheless, its members' sojourns have brought back many fantastic secrets and powerful weapons. Spies infiltrating Citadel Enferac have reported that this portal is no longer the only one within Mount Nyisaid, and nor is it the most terrifying.
535085488:
Sombrefell Hall

The massive manor known as Sombrefell Hall is located about 25 miles northeast of Rozenport in Ustalav. It is situated at the end of a fairly well demarcated road abutting the Lantern Lake. The manor has belonged to the Oscilar family for generations. It is built in the gothic Ustalavic fashion and its grand entrance consists of mahogany double doors.
541854578:
Union of Carpenters, Stonemasons, and Woodcutters

The Union of Carpenters, Stonemasons, and Woodcutters is located near the center of The Coins district, where the city's finest craftspeople gather and where commissions for projects requiring their talent are received.
548164298:
Pulura's Fall

Pulura's Fall is a former waterfall located in Sarkoris near the Sarkoris Scar, northeast of the ruins of Iz. Maybe one of the only places not fully controlled by demons in the Wounded Lands while the Worldwound was open, a temple of Pulura led by the powerful cleric Eliandra stood in the dried-up waterfall.
548848878:
Halidon

Halidon is a small logging community on the edge of the northern Backar Forest in Molthune. Lying roughly halfway between the capital of Canorate and Braganza, the town is a frequent stop for travelers and has, therefore, a large number of inns with respect to its resident population.
554060648:
Wispil

Located in the depths of the Verduran Forest, Wispil is the gnomish city responsible for most of Taldor's supply of timber and much of its wood craft. Wispil provides the timber that fuels the bustling Imperial Naval Shipyards of Cassomir, which in turn grant the Taldan Imperial Navy its power.
556465668:
Chiron

Chiron is a small Katapeshi town on the small island of Sand Haven.
558365338:
Dbede

The tower of Dbede is 100 feet tall and 50 feet wide and was built by termites. It dominates the skyline near the center of the Screaming Jungle in the southern Mwangi Expanse, and early explorers mistakenly assumed it marked the site of an ancient lost city. Millions of termites inhabit the tower, and they fiercely defend their home against any trespassers, as is evidenced by the thousands of clean-picked skeletons that litter the ground around it. The Screaming Jungle's intelligent inhabitants occasionally use the termites' ferocity to execute captives in a truly gruesome manner. Hundreds of similar termite mounds can be found on the Korir Plains north of the jungle, but none compares in size to Dbede.

580944598:
Dortrot Ranch

Dortrot Ranch is a large horse ranch located on the plains of the eastern Gravelands just south of the Path River. The family of the owner, Chap "Old Man" Dortrot, has been raising both draft and warhorses there for 11 generations.
581087618:
Ilsurian

Ilsurian is a small agricultural and fishing town situated on the northern banks of Lake Syrantula at the mouth of the Skull River. The town is on the Yondabakari Road, 64 miles northeast of Whistledown (and a total of 359 miles east of the city of Magnimar). The Yondabakari Road continues towards the town of Biston, 45 miles to the east.
581532218:
Valley of the Birthing Death

The Valley of the Birthing Death is located in the foothills of the Stormspear Mountains in the far north-east of Icemark in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The valley looks as if it had been hewed clean out of the mountain and its narrow red walls create a realm of perpetual twilight. The Varki tribes avoid the valley due to the curse of Birthing Death: a curse manifesting as a rapid rejuvenation of the afflicted taking them back to childhood and then oblivion. This curse is not just physical but mental as well; stealing the memories and experience of the victim as they regress. Many have attempted to study or harness this strange effect but they have all succumbed to it, the knowledge required to understand the strange curse is inevitably one of the first things the curse claims.
590562518:
Fort Benbem

Fort Benbem is a small port fortress on the Pangalley Atoll in the Shackles. It has been the home port of the Screaming Reaver since the ship was banned from using even Shark Island ports. Black-Eye Benbem had the fortress built using slaves that he drowned soon after its completion. Fort Benbem consists of no more than a dozen wooden buildings surrounded by a log palisade, and is hidden by thick palm trees.
592592568:
The Pit

The Pit is a forgotten complex from Thassilon used as a research base by Karzoug agents to spy on their enemies Sorshen and especially Alaznist. It was built at a point where the energies of the Outer Rifts were strongest, though the researchers didn't know why.
598895918:
Torrentmoot

Torrentmoot was a camp established on a flat hill so that dignitaries from various nations and factions could observe Belkzen's orc culture and potentially forge diplomatic paths. The initiative was spearheaded by Ardax after Tar-Baphon's forces attempted to subdue the orcs once again. The camp's name derives from its proximity to a vantage point for observing the Flood Road when it inundates annually.

During the flood of 4725 AR, the region's necromantic energies surged, unleashing a horde of undead and those gathered at the camp were forced to evacuate.
602565448:
Devil's Elbow Harbor

The Devil's Elbow Harbor is located in the only safe place for a ship to dock on the island, as the rest of the coast is full of reefs. The island's inhabitants built piers and buildings here, which are now just ruins and abandoned stone walls.
604095468:
Temple of Lost Coins

The Temple of Lost Coins is a half-ruined former church of Trudd located in the Coins district of Absalom. It is the base of operations of the Forthright, the thieves' guild of the Coins. It is also the residence of the guild master known as the Harlequin.
610455698:
Kembe

The ruins of Kembe are in the southern part of the Mwangi Jungle, in the Mwangi Expanse.

It consists of three concentric rings of buildings. The buildings of the inner ring are sized for giants, those of the middle ring are sized for humans, and those of the outer ring are sized for halflings.

According to the myths carved on the city walls, a race of giants arrived from the sky in silver craft. They built the inner ring of buildings, but over generations found themselves getting smaller and smaller, requiring the building of the middle ring, and then finally the outer ring. They eventually shrunk too small to be seen.
621728718:
Foxglove Manor

Foxglove Manor is a haunted mansion on the western coast of Varisia (also known as the Lost Coast), approximately halfway between Magnimar and Sandpoint. Constructed in 4624 AR by minor Magnimarian noble Vorel Foxglove, and funded by the Brotherhood of the Seven, Foxglove Manor was intended to be the ancestral home of Foxglove's family for 100 years before reverting to the Brotherhood's ownership. Three generations of Foxgloves have met tragic ends in the Manor, from Vorel and his wife to Aldern Foxglove, who perished in 4707 AR. The manor consists of three stories overlooking a high sea cliff, a basement and natural cave network, and a ruined outbuilding.

As of 4710 AR, Foxglove Manor is believed to still be haunted by Vorel's malign spirit and is currently uninhabited.

Kaer Maga

641048018:
Paupers' Graves

Paupers' Graves is a small graveyard three miles east of Sandpoint along the old Varisian Kaspakari (traditional coastal route along the Lost Coast). It was used as a graveyard by the poor of Sandpoint and the laborers from Magnimar who built the town. Usage dwindled once the Sandpoint Boneyard was opened to all regardless of ability to pay.

Several years ago Jediah Kheln robbed the cemetery of its medallion of the Empyreal Lord Ashava. Kanker, ghoul priest of Kabriri, tricked Kheln into this theft. Kheln was repaid by Kanker with his death and transformation into a ghoul. Since then Kheln has turned the graveyard into his personal domain, building an underground warren for himself, his ghoul thralls, and his feral ghoul hounds.

The warrens extend throughout the region, reaching under Devil's Platter and even to the old smuggler's tunnels beneath Sandpoint. Kanker presides over an ancient temple to Kabriri somewhere in the tunnels.
656396128:
Elkhaven Lodge

Elkhaven Lodge was a hunting lodge dedicated to Erastil in the Fangwood, though it fell into ruin when the Darkblight spread through the forest in 4062 AR. Rangers and freedom fighters have used the lodge as a hideout sporadically, but no one has rebuilt the place due to the conflict with Molthune.
673790948:
Sirola Manor

Sirola Manor is a spacious building in Westgate, but not as ostentatious as some others in the city, being the residence of retired Pathfinder Venla and Urmas Sirola.
675468508:
Winter Garden

Owned by a former member of the Umbral Court, the Winter Garden is a cottage in the western Uskwood whose land around it is haunted by disorienting mists and unending snowfall.
676422368:
Cloudreaver Keep

An immense fortress called Cloudreaver Keep guards the eastern border of the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar. It is one of the three points connected by the massive Bridge of the Gods, the other two being Alkenstar City and Dongun Hold on the far side of the canyon. While the keep was intended to serve as a defense against outside threats, since its construction it has been plagued almost constantly with hauntings from ghosts within and attacks from nearby bands of yetis.

The keep stands on the western bank of the Ustradi River, and all trade from Nex passes through it.
684172758:
Mount Rein

Mount Rein is a large, snow-topped mountain in the western end of the Fog Peaks, on the border between the nations of Galt and Taldor. It is used as a guide to the border between those two nations.

Ilizmagorti

702199108:
Ulizmila's Cottage

Ulizmila's Cottage is located somewhere in the Darkmoon Wood, but the locals prefer not to know its location, for those who seek it never return. It is said that Ulizmila, a daughter of Baba Yaga, resides in the cottage, and while legends tell of wonders offered to those who visit, these tales invariably end in tragedy, where the price to pay for her services proves too high.
718828038:
Vira Ciucci

Vira Ciucci is the mansion of the House Ciucci, a noble family of Westcrown, their home a fortress against thieves and criminals.
724258228:
Porthmos Prison

Porthmos Prison is located in Porthmos Prefecture in eastern Taldor, at the foot of the World's Edge Mountains, on the edge of the Whistling Plains and near the town of Sardis.
730765488:
Erekrus

Erekrus is a subterranean necropolis in southern Osirion, west of Mount Osiki. It is built on the side of the mountain and serves as a necropolis for pharaohs who were noble and worthy for Pahmet's blessings. In Ancient Osirion it was a great honor to be blessed by the Pahmet.

Two of the pharaohs who earned the blessings of the Pahmet of Erekrus were Osezis II and Menedes XVII. Osezis II became famous for his policies which ended a nationwide famine. He was entombed in Erekrus in -652 AR. Menedes XVII earned Pahmet's favor because he managed to abolish the slavery system during his reign. He was entombed in Erekrus in 1262 AR.

Erekrus is ruled by a pair of priests who claim to be siblings, Garzukk and Trelka. Together they guide the Pahmet in their prayers and rituals to honor the deceased god-kings of Osirion. They have disparate features for siblings as Garzukk who is the older of the two, has long golden locks of hair and bronze skin while Trelka is dour with dusky skin.

Greengold

747291668:
House of Ensland

The House of Ensland is one of the two most notorious ruins of noble houses and estates in the blasted wasteland of the Furrows, along with Candlehalls.

The house is located four leagues from the ruins of the village of Feldgrau amidst the vast and blackened trench system called the Dead Man's Maze. The estate is almost certainly haunted, as ghost lights are seen playing around the grounds and other-worldly gamblers frequent these noble houses trying to entrap wanderers across the charred lands.

One unsuspecting gambler was Lecit Ghazts, a greedy knight officer who gambled away his body to a devil in the House of Ensland. Ghazts' head still rolls around the Furrows looking for his, or someone else's, body.
747527648:
Rhet's Home

Rhet's Home is an orphanage in the northern part of Mudhaven. It appears to be an ordinary house, but the happy laughter of children playing in the yard can be heard. The dromaar Rhet Tafmar founded it and cares for the children with great dedication, finding them adoptive families and enjoying a very good reputation in the district. Some speculate that he must have been an orphan himself, but in reality, he is the son of a successful merchant from Katapesh who uses the funds generously given to him by his family to maintain the orphanage.
760526748:
Gadka's Magical Oddities

761489088:
Zurakan

Zurakan is the walled capital of the nation of Hukaris that sits at the foot of Mount Jabel in the centre of the country.
765495858:
House of 1,000 Silks

The House of 1,000 Silks is a silk-producing facility in northwestern Quain in Tian Xia known for its magically-infused garments. The secrets of its trade are carefully protected by the martial artists who own the establishment.
766678098:
Salav

Salav is a Qadiran city located in the Northern Zho Mountains area, sitting at the head waters of the River Ladan in the foothills of the Zho Mountains. The Northern Zho Mountains region is the poorest of all Qadira's regions and faces the neighbouring land of Taldor just across the Jalrune River, as such it is the least hungry for war with the proud kingdom.
780531628:
Mirivenn

Mirivenn is a settlement of aquatic elves in the Arcadian Ocean, located a few hundred miles off the coast of Ravounel west of the Dismal Nitch.
780540578:
Koor

Koor is a ruined forge-city found in the northern part of Qadira in the Zho Mountains. Once known for producing incredible weapons and armor that were shipped throughout the Inner Sea region, Koor was abandoned in the 41st century AR.
785854378:
Manaket

2 locations:
  • Cave of Tiandra
  • Qassabah Heb Amar
807748448:
Little Oppara

Little Oppara is the only settlement on the Taldas Isle in the Shackles. The little town was a small pirate haven but, in 4656 AR, Danziul Maxeme took control of the island and remade the town to mirror the opulence of far-off Taldor. Little Oppara's fine taverns serve delicacies from across Golarion, and richly attired men and women frequent plays and operas at the famed Blooming Rose Theater.
818524758:
Obrigan Gate

The Obrigan Gate is the only obvious access point between Rego Crua and Rego Cader, a massive gate built by House Oberigo centuries ago to control the passage of beggars into the rest of the city.
826491538:
Canorate

Canorate is the capital of the nation of Molthune. It is an impressive city which manages to combine architectural splendor with defensive fortifications. It is said that even the poor of Canorate live in luxury, and to a certain extent this is true—citizens allow a select number of necessary laborers to live in fine accommodation (but naturally kept separate from the rest of the population) in order to encourage them to settle in the city.

The city's most exclusive district is known as the Sweet Orchard. Non-residents are not allowed to enter this exclusive section without a valid work or travel permit.

The Imperial Castle dominates the center of the city. The ruler of Molthune, Imperial Governor Markwin Teldas, maintains an impressive war room within the castle. Pride of place is given to a large scale model of the Inner Sea region, on which the generals plot their next campaigns.
831052338:
Valley of the Chosen Children

The Valley of the Chosen Children is a canyon of the Naurepzi River in Xopatl after it flows away from the Follower's River. Locals claim that at night the laughter of dozens of children echoes through the valley, and that anyone who sails the entire length of the Valley of the Chosen Children in a single night receives some kind of blessing.
854584828:
Belhaim

Belhaim is a small town located on a tributary of the Verduran Fork in the nation of Taldor's Verduran Forest.
858838588:
Roshik

Roshik is a town in Almhult located on the southern Steaming Sea coast of Cape Almhult, between Asre to the west and Helsingbyen to the east.
878845198:
Rosehaven

Rosehaven is a small Galtan village located on the East Sellen River south of the River Kingdoms city of Liberthane. They celebrate an annual three-day autumn festival known as Mercylight, commemorating the community's defeat of a small coven of Gyronna-worshipers in 4684 AR.
889582808:
Okeno

The harbor city of Okeno, located on the southern coast of Stonespine Island off the eastern shore of Katapesh, is the third-largest city in Katapesh. Also known as the Yellow City, its bustling slave markets host up to 1,000 slavers, buyers, and travelers at any given time and is the largest slave market in the Inner Sea region.
910064048:
Baakai

The town of Baakai is the largest human settlement in Shenmen.
920265128:
Jungle Runners

Jungle Runners is a shop where Erandlon and Bujkis Tallwoman offer their services as guides for explorers or nobles who wish to venture into the jungle, whether to find a ruin or bring a dinosaur skull back home. No one knows their way around the dangerous inland jungle better than they do.
921305778:
Valanyne

Valanyne is a town located in the far east of Kyonin, near the Sellen River. The village was unremarkable until Godsrain, which severed all communication. Anyone who has gone looking for the town or its inhabitants has returned empty-handed.

Chalk Harbor

950104488:
Hope Lost

The Hope Lost mine in the Irrisen province of Wintercrux produces the rare blue quartz crystals known as ice diamonds that are notoriously arduous to excavate. Many forced to work here by the White Witches or Cold Sisters die quickly due to the mine's brutal conditions.

The vicious Countess Grinnelise, whose palace is carved into the face of the mountain, oversees the mine. Enslaved miners prefer the daily beatings of the ice troll gang bosses over being summoned to the countess' palace, where the screams of victims during her notorious orgies of cannibalism and torture are unbearable.

At some point recent to 4712 AR, some slaves escaped using a secret tunnel and the help of yetis who dwell in a large warren deep in the mountains. The slaves gave stolen ice diamonds to the yetis in thanks.
953276798:
Broadfoot Marshals Station House

Broadfoot Marshals Station House is a base for a private firefighting brigade in The Docks district of Absalom. Under the instruction of the dwarven engineer Bagara Broadfoot, they use water carts that, through pedals, can create pressure to expel water and extinguish fires, which are quite dangerous in a district made almost entirely out of wood.
954724088:
Raven's Watch

Raven's Watch was a fort constructed on the northern banks of the Turandarok River in the Ravenroost tors of Varisia's Sandpoint Hinterlands. The prison was abandoned when Sandpoint grew large enough to support its own garrison and jail, and rumours about a haunting or a curse have swirled around the prison since.

Though the above-ground portion of the prison quickly crumbled into ruin, the lower portions, where the guards were said to have left some prisoners, remain largely intact. A band of bandits aligned with the cult of Lamashtu are now said to have taken up watch within the prison's ruins.
955578698:
Haven

Haven is a secluded and tranquil village nestled within a hidden valley to the north of Hillcross in the Tusk Mountains. Unusually, the village is populated by both Kellids and giants who live peacefully together, and who also work together on the terraced farms built into the nearby slopes. Its inhabitants dwell in crude stone structures characterized by monastic simplicity, and the streets regularly see the presence of strangely calm dire bears, smilodons, witchwargs, mammoths, and mastodons.
957172268:
Yargos's Mission

Yargos's Mission is the most reliable charitable organization in The Puddles, where Yargos Gill, the old veteran, puts a portion of his fortune to provide food and medicine to the most unfortunate in the district. Unfortunately, that includes basically the entire district, and the workers have their hands full running the charity while also having to watch out for the most nefarious elements who seek to get their hands on their resources.
961068928:
Vangeline Pass

The Vangeline Pass in the Shattered Range provides entrance into a remote portion of western Geb from the eastern Mwangi Expanse. It is one of the three major passes from the Mwangi Expanse to the east: Kho-Rarne Pass, the Ndele Gap, and the Vangeline Pass. The pass is rarely used because there no major cities in the eastern Mwangi Expanse close to the Vangeline Pass and also the usual dangers of the mountains and the jungles are augmented by monsters migrating south from the Mana Wastes, or dragging themselves out of Draxmere, as well as monsters from the Axanir River to the south.
966253758:
Black Mask

The Black Mask is a high-end costume shop in Absalom's Ascendant Court, carrying masks, cloaks, and everything from common disguise kits to costumes used by parade or theater performers. Behind this facade, the Black Mask is a temple to the god Norgorber, offering worship once the shop's doors close at the end of the business day. Assassins and rogues of all descriptions find that these clandestine meetings and the masked identities of many patrons makes the Black Mask a superb meeting spot for all manner of shady deals. Locals are frightened when the Black Mask starts selling skinsaws (leather costumes with ghoulish faces) because these "sales" always seem to precede strings of gruesome murders, though these events have never been officially connected.
966673788:
Willowmourn

Willowmourn is the estate of the ruling family of the Ustalavic county of Amaans, the Galdanas, and can be found on the western border of Lake Kavapesta. A sprawling possession, it contains topiary gardens, hedge mazes, a bountiful garden, not to mention a well-built and secure manor house. Rumors of wild debauchery there have persisted for a long time, although recently the gossip has taken a decidedly more sinister tone. Now gossips whisper of fearful hauntings and murder most foul.
973908118:
Shadun

The ruins of the Qadiran town of Shadun are located in the eastern foothills of the Zho Mountains, on the opposite side of the mountain range from Gurat. The hillsides around the town were once covered in terrace farms watered by the tributaries of the Pashman River, and criss-crossed with green trails. Tragedy struck when the nearby volcanoes Zhonar and Zhobl began spewing great clouds of dust and ash that covered the countryside, forcing the town's inhabitants to flee. No one is quite sure what happened to the citizens of Shadun, and today its desolate streets are stalked at night by strange shadowy figures with eyes like glowing embers.
974260188:
Caliphas

A city made rich by trade from across Lake Encarthan, Caliphas has long been described as the very soul of Ustalav. Caliphas is Ustalav's largest city and has a tremendous impact on the surrounding lands, supporting most of the wineries and perfumeries that dot the County of Caliphas.

985418878:
Chelish Fleet Wreckage

The Chelish Fleet Wreckage lies dozens of miles off the coast of Vidrian, having been Cheliax's response in 4643 AR to Sargava's independence and an attempt to bring it back into the empire. The Free Captains pirates, who were under Sargava's control, attacked the fleet, sinking it at sea. A sedacthy tribe is using the wreckage as a base from which they attack coastal settlements.
987624328:
Steel Maridoth

The Steel Maridoth is the Possum Gang's base of operations in the Sodden Lands. Since the arrival of the Eye of Abendego, a multitude of ships have been adrift in the region, and these often gather and collide with each other, forming the Steel Maridoth, which gets its name from the large number of metal ships that congregate there.
996332168:
Nazilli

The ruins of the town of Nazilli are located on the southern edges of the Verduran Forest in Taldor. The settlement was destroyed by the black dragon Aeteperax during a period of Taldan history known as the Dragon Plague.

1007682128:
Averaka

The town of Averaka is located on the southeastern coast of the island of Flintyreach in the Linnorm Kingdom of the Ironbound Islands and is predominantly populated by dromaars.
1013016208:
Temple of the Elk

The Temple of the Elk is an overgrown and forgotten shrine to Erastil in the northern Narlmarches. A dense growth of brambles and other thorny plants envelops the expansive clearing where it is situated, deep within the forest.
1054915338:
Tomb of the Iron Medusa

The Tomb of the Iron Medusa is the underground final resting place of House Adella, a noble house of Taldor that once was among the empire's most wealthy and extravagant houses. They descended into diabolism and depravity, and the house was dissolved within the span of a single generation during the 43rd century AR.
1055495078:
Wreck of the Brine Demon

The Brine Demon was the ship of the Free Captain Avret Kinkarian. Kinkarian and the Brine Demon menaced Desperation Bay and the southeastern Arcadian Ocean, and were part of a scheme to extort the government of Sargava. The ship crashed into a cliffside of Smuggler's Shiv at some point prior to 4710 AR.
1060720898:
Scar Thicket

Scar Thicket is a forbidding overgrown area deep in the namesake Scarhusrt region of the Whisperwood of Cheliax, tucked between the flows of the Doracium, Malvesa, and Iseld rivers.

Infrequently visited because of its evil reputation, this area remains a primal woodland and frequently features in hushed conversations and outlandish stories. Everything from monstrous [[insect]s, deformed animals, and enormous bats are rumored to emerge from its depths. Youths in the area grow up learning to deal with evil outsiders, and the inhabitants are quite skilled at leading hunts for them.
1065660638:
Hall of Holies

The Hall of Holies is home to the Chamber of Ecclestials, the oldest district council in all Absalom and is even older than the Grand Council of Absalom. The Chamber of Ecclestials is made up of twenty members of prominent religions who meet within the hall once a month spending most of their time organizing and arranging holidays, festivals, celebrations, religious observances and protests between the near countless religions of the Ascendant Court, many of whom hate each other with a truly religious zeal. While keeping these seething cauldron of religious intrigue from boiling over is now a relatively mundane duty for the council they do sometimes meet in the event of a true crisis. Hall of Holies is a former temple of the dead god Aroden. In between meetings, the Hall of Holies is home to the bureaucrats and administrators who actually keep the Ascendant Court district running.
1065928958:
Langkhu

Langkhu is a city of inland Goka, near a gap in the Wall of Heaven in western Tian Xia.
1079507358:
Holy Xatramba

The ruins of Holy Xatramba (sometimes also spelled Xatremba) lie in the eastern jungle of the Mwangi Expanse, southwest of the city of Kibwe.

The city was founded in 3699 AR, at the site of Volnagur's first defeat (which happened in 909 AR). Its religion was an unique mix of ancestor worship and worship of Pharasma and its memory is revered by the Mwangi people.
1079799268:
Augustana

Augustana is an Inner Sea port city in the nation of Andoran located on Aspo Bay. It is the second-largest city in the nation, and boasts one of the largest shipyards of the Inner Sea region.
1081539788:
Red Drake Warehouses

Red Drake Warehouses is a series of warehouses in The Docks run by Ptarnex Dexarion, the ruthless boss of Ogrekin Hall. He runs the business by offering merchants space to store whatever they want, with the caveat that if they fail to pay for even a single month, all the merchandise inside becomes his property. He makes sure his customers are aware of this situation and has made more money selling confiscated goods than renting the space. It is said that he uses infernal contracts and hired thieves to steal from merchants so they can't pay on time, but since the rent is so low, many take the risk.
1084042348:
Gloaming Arbor

Gloaming Arbor is a region of the western Verduran Forest with an intense connection to the Netherworld.
1089802518:
Beast

3 locations:

1095082848:
First Step

First Step is a massive fortress located at the heart of the Exalted Wood in the nation of Razmiran. Here, new recruits to the Church of Razmir are trained to become missionaries for placement both at home and abroad.
1098850868:
Dim Gate

The Dim Gate is an interplanetary gate deep beneath a peak in the Napsune Mountains of the eastern Sodden Lands. It is under construction by the lich diviner Meyi Panaho under the patronage of a mysterious being calling himself Tzriek, a bone sage from the dead planet Eox. Tzriek convinced Meyi to become immortal through lichdom in order to have the time to unlock the secrets of the universe.

A narrow trail winds up the mountain's peak to the Dim Gate complex's observatory, which overlooks a narrow, secluded valley. The mountains protect the observatory from the perpetually rotating arms of the Eye of Abendego that deluge the lowlands of the Sodden Lands, and the observatory's elevation provides a clear view of the sky on most nights. Skeletal champions patrol the grounds around the observatory, and a nightwing called Gloomtide soars above the site keeping watch for intruders.
1102837608:
Vault of the Forgotten Prophet

The Vault of the Forgotten Prophet is a vault hidden under the plains of Rhuzam that can only be accessed by using teleportation magic.
1111405458:
Nature's Glass

Nature's Glass is a small, natural pool of water deep within the Fangwood and to the west of Crossfen. Its surface reflects like a mirror, and a legend has grown up around it that the water reflects the true nature of those who gaze into it. This phenomenon has attracted the attention of some fey, among them Leeta, a swan maiden determined to protect the waters by drowning those who try to poison them.
1116814548:
Stepped Tower of Djedefar

The Stepped Tower of Djedefar is a site in northern Osirion. It is located on the north eastern tip of the Alamein Peninsula. The tower is home to a community of Irori-worshiping monks.

1123699468:
Ullerskad

Ullerskad is an Ulfen city situated on the northern banks of the Rimeflow River in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. It is a major settlement in the region known as the Thanelands and a hub of trade and commerce in the region. Many of the wealthiest Ulfen live in the city, which is known for its popularity among retired Huscarls of the Ulfen Guard who have returned from their well-paid services to the emperor of Taldor.

Numerous hunting lodges dot the surrounding region, and hunting the local wild boars (and the troublesome wolves that prey on the farmers' flocks) is a popular pastime.

Ullerskad is the location of a gold-adorned temple to Gorum. The nearby stream and grove of trees is the site of sacrifice, especially during the spring equinox.
1130342208:
Crowhollow

Crowhollow is a sinkhole in the Sellen Hills of northeastern Numeria, and the site of a piece of the Divinity that fell during the Rain of Stars. It is also the home of a half-mechanical lich named Alling Third, a former Technic League agent who sought immortality in order to fully exploit its technology.
1133886318:
Devildrome

The Devildrome is a semi-legal summoning arena where two mages pit their summoned beings against either other summoned beings or gladiators. Betting is the primary source of income, and locals enjoy the violence commonplace here.
1135302608:
Breachill

19 locations:
  • Citadel Altaerein
  • Shelyn's Smile
  • Great Dreamhouse
  • Mount Sinatuk
  • Posandi Bros.
  • Wizard's Grace
  • Breach Creek Lumber
  • Breachill Archives
  • Pickled Ear
  • and 10 others…
1152292558:
Dammartorp

Dammartorp is a small settlement on the Marbleflow River in Irrisen's Hoarwood province. It is jointly ruled by the Jadwigas Tereza and Tsviata, twin sisters locked in a struggle for control of the town.

The peasantry are divided between the two baronesses and they even frequent different markets and taverns and spit on the ground when passing one another. The peasant followers of Lady Tereza are led by Viglund Lyubienko, who is also the owner of the Tavern on the Water, while those who favor Lady Tsviata are led by the prominent wool seller Zora Oblatov.

Bandits infest the trade route between Dammartorp and Hoarwood but the baronesses are too engaged in their quarrel to address the problem.
1182857398:
Tintavex's Aerie

Tintavex's Aerie is located in the southern part of the Mindspin Mountains, overlooking the Umbral Basin from the cliffs. The half-dragon Tintavex nests there. She is very territorial about her nest and attacks anyone who approaches.
1190935728:
Temple Row

Temple Row is a neighborhood teeming with temples to various deities, most of them related to the sea and weather, located in The Docks. The temple of Gozreh is near tide pools that fluctuate with the high ot low tide, generating a good amount of wind, and the area is favored by water druids. Those unfamiliar with the faith consider Besmara's Boardwalk to be its temple, but that's a stretch, as true followers gather in a seedy tavern with a tradition of throwing out customers who lose bets by tossing a hand in the air as an offering. A group of tengus erected a temple to Hei Feng on the second floor of a warehouse that was struck by lightning twice during a storm. After convincing the owner, they repaired the roof and can now live there rent-free.
1192750368:
Devil's Rest

The Devil's Rest is a centuries-old trading post located on the southern edge of the Sarkoris Scar in Sarkoris. Its name is said to be derived from its purported founder, a Chelaxian noble.
1195129028:
Crouching Jackal

Located in northern Katapesh, the Crouching Jackal is a huge statue that stands nearly seventy feet tall and appears to depcit a jackal, although the body looks more like that of a cat (or possibly an Osirian sphinx). The head has the upright, pointed ears of a jackal and a jackal's bushy tail.

No one knows who built the Crouching Jackal or why, though some rumours claim that if a person sleeps in front of it, they will be transformed into a jackalwere. Other theories include the Crouching Jackal being a hollow and unopened treasure vault, or a vast construct used in ancient wars as a terrifying weapon. Many also believe that it is a sister statue to the lizard-like Creeping Watcher of the Vargas Swamp. Regardless of its original purpose, the Crouching Jackal can be seen for miles around and is used as a convenient landmark by local travellers.
1203261878:
Founder's Peak

Founder's Peak is located on the east bank of Southrun, being the highest point in the area. It was named after Founder Adel, who is said to have cut down the trees he found there to create a boat to cross the river, and from its heights saw how the city should be built. The area is full of farms and olive orchards tended by generations of druids of the Arthrugge family, who maintain political neutrality.
1240282118:
Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval

Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval is located in a large warehouse along the docks of Katapesh and does exactly what its name suggests. Founded by the adventurous sailors Darg Jexler and Wilbur Young, nowadays they limit themselves to wreck salvaging and tourist tours, they also sell items useful to adventurers heading to aquatic environments. Whenever news reaches Katapesh of a wrecked ship or nearby sunken treasure Jexler and Young are normally the first to investigate. Their ship the Iron Mermaid is specially designed for salvage operations and is a common sight in the water around the city. Currently word has reached them of a new opportunity, the Star Runner was attacked relatively nearby by a huge sea monster suspected to be a kraken. Supposedly the cargo holds of the Star Runner were stuffed to the brim with trade goods and ancient relics from the Mwangi Expanse. Jexler & Young are desperate to get there before the expedition from Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie who are after the sea monster but are none the less rivals.
1248630118:
Almas

3 locations:
  • Archstone Monument
  • Citadel of the Supreme Elect
  • Palace of the People's Council
1248698008:
Palace of Virtue

The Palace of Virtue is a monastery in eastern Molthune, located in the northern foothills of the Menador Mountains. It houses several bronze dragons in human guise who act as advisors to its leaders.
1250969568:
Tomb of the Thousand Tusks

The Tomb of the Thousand Tusks is a crypt located in the southernmost Barrier Wall mountains on the border between the Mwangi Expanse and the Sodden Lands. It is believed to be the lair of the adhukait asura Kaisadvaisad, who guards the skull of the empyreal lord Shei Five Dawns.
1271529278:
Salhar Estate

The Salhar Estate is where the Grand Ambassador of Osirion, Dremdhet Salhar, lives surrounded by walls and iron gates, with a personal guard patrolling the area to prevent intruders from entering.
1276142998:
Senghor

The port city-state of Senghor is located on the Bay of Senghor, which is part of the Fever Sea on Garund's western coast. It is on the western end of the peninsula known as the Kaava Lands (the kaava are a pygmy tribe of kech) in the southern Mwangi Expanse.
1281952338:
Oakrib Inn

Oakrib Inn is the site where the Silver Ravens negotiated the details of Ravounel's secession from Cheliax. This large inn near the head of the Yolubilis River was once a popular stopping point for those traveling through the Menador Gap. Since the destruction of Menador Keep and the closure of the Gap, the inn's owner Olradi Ormovor has reinvented it as a luxury tourist destination and historical heritage site, complete with dramatic reenactments of the tense negotiations.
1283218578:
Sureshire Ranch

The Sureshire Ranch is a horse ranch located in the Ligos Prefecture of Taldor with a storied history and national fame. According to Sureshire family, the first horse was bred here as a cross between a deer and a unicorn. Fanciful tales aside, the Sureshire Ranch has a millennia-long pedigree of horse breeding, and both the nation's aristocracy and Taldor's military favour its horses. Unlike the hulking brutes of the nearby Stavian Ranches, the horses of Sureshire are agile and quick, more akin to the horses favoured in the neighbouring kingdom of Qadira (whom the Sureshire family claims stole these horse from them).

The last of the Sureshire family died over six centuries ago. As of 4717 AR, the ranch is run by a council of Taldan and halfling families who proudly sponsor the Emperor's Mile, a gruelling horse race across the Ligos Prefecture that the ranch has sponsored for 1,200 years. Its winner is not only showered in gold but gains access to Well of Kurgess, a spring whose water enhances any beast of burden that drinks from it.
1299549758:
Tempering Hall

The Tempering Hall is a combat school located across the main street from the Seventh Church in the Ascendant Court district of Absalom. The facility traditionally trains paladins of Iomedae as well as those called by Abadar, Irori, and Shelyn, a few of whom supplement the dozens of paladins of Iomedae trained by the elderly priests. However, in recent years, the Hall has started accepting anyone dedicated to fighting evil. A specialism of the school is teaching mastery of a god's favoured weapon to that deity's followers.
1302285418:
Merry Pranksters

The Merry Pranksters is a group of two dozen pirates who survive on the island of Hesmene's Grotto in the Shackles.
1311014048:
Moonless Mirror

The Moonless Mirror is a relic of the Everwar with Cheliax, a sealed portal to the Netherworld that sits in the cliffs overlooking Nisroch Bay and seethes with magical energy.
1321450118:
Sauerton

Sauerton is a settlement in northeastern Andoran in the Carpenden Plains region, located between the banks of the Andoshen River and the Verduran Forest.
1326420848:
Bolgrad

Bolgrad is an underground mining city in the northeastern Five Kings Range that specializes in gemstones; the cavern city's gem-rich ceiling earned it the nickname "Star-Studded Bolgrad". Most of its majority dwarven population works either in the mining and processing of gems or as jewelers.
1332681948:
Uldani Necropolis

The Uldani Necropolis is a site in the desert interior of Thuvia. It lies not far from the city of Pashow.
1333697578:
Greengold

5 locations:
  • Founder's Square
  • Hedgerow Holloway
  • Laughing Gale
  • Steelhead Pier
  • Temple of Calistria
1337233948:
The Slace Block

3 locations:
  • House of Whips
  • The Scorpion's Sting
  • Trillia's Bathhouse
1338392798:
Skarx's Prison

Skarx's Prison was used by the Council of Thieves in 4710 AR as a place to hold the most sensitive prisoners instead of placing them under house arrest, as was the practice elsewhere. A cambion named Skarx ran the prison with a discipline and cruelty that rivaled that of a devil.
1339378688:
Jolizpan

Jolizpan is a city located in the extreme northwest of Xopatl, near Tumbaja Mountain, the nexus of Xopatl's Veins of Creation.
1345169788:
Farnvale

Farnvale is an isolated town with a dark secret, located deep in Galt's Boarwood, where the infamous Gray Gardeners never visited.
1367738438:
Tar Kuata

The monastic settlement of Tar Kuata, dedicated to the god Irori, is located among the peaks of the Barrier Wall in southwestern Osirion near the Footprints of Rovagug, and the borders of Thuvia and the Mwangi Expanse. The Monastery of Tar Kuata was founded by Narmek Tar Kuata, a Sothan priest of Irori now venerated as a saint.

Many among the Pahmet, the sand dwarves, claim that Tar Kuata is home to some of their contemplative brethren, the Ouat, who are scholars of the martial arts taught in the monastery. The Ouat are in fact an isolated caste of dwarven monks based in the mountain valley near Tar Kuata. They are led by Menkha Helg.
1376513898:
Heibarr

The ruins of Heibarr are located on a branch of the Sellen River in the River Kingdoms, downstream of Mormouth in Pitax and upstream of Outsea.
1392310898:
Undervale

Undervale is a way station in the Umbral Basin that also serves as a safe haven for the Bellflower Network. The area is teeming with predators and potential trouble, but it also offers an opportunity for those seeking to escape Cheliax or Nidal. Constructs and illusions protect the station, and part of it is built underground. Its residents are mostly halflings whose cheerful and welcoming nature is a warm welcome.

The leader of Undervale is undoubtedly Landry Tethertine, who maintains the constructs and seeks new members to join the station.
1410008768:
Coldwater

Coldwater is a small town on the western bank of the Gullik River in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold. Baroness Wilimina is the ruler of the town.

The peasants of Coldwater look with envy the changing seasons in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords but no peasant is allowed to cross the river. They have to work the docks and house and entertain the merchants traveling up and down the Gullik River. Some young and rebellious Jadwiga have formed a stilyagi gang and call themselves the Lords of the Tusk. They are led by Paval Turosky, a foolish youth, and enjoy crossing the river and imitate the Kellid barbarians. On some occasions the Lords wander deep into the foreign lands and they say they want to forage deeper into the wilds to hunt bison and the prehistoric animals that roam those lands.
1410278708:
Highpoint Tower

Highpoint Tower is an abandoned garrison and watch point located close to the capital of Bildt on the island of Aegos, in the Linnorm Kingdom of Broken Bay. As of 4711 AR, it was the home of a tribe of winter wolves and worgs.
1415543538:
Plumetown

2 locations:
  • Cauldrons of Calm and Calamity
  • Mount Keeba
1418092148:
Wracked Rock

The Wracked Rock is a stony structure in an abandoned block of buildings in the Precipice Quarter, inhabited not out of necessity but because they are drawn by the strange dreams that occur when sleeping near the obelisk. Thousands of years ago, a Rovagug cultist summoned a giant monster to destroy the city, but heroes managed to turn it to stone before it could ravage the neighborhood, killing it by shattering it into pieces. The only part that remained was this rock, a tusk protruding from the head that lies buried underground. After the earthquake a few decades ago, a small opening opened into the subsoil, from which all who have ventured never return. Rovagug cultists are often seen in the area, but since their violence doesn't spread beyond the block, the Knights of Lastwall see no reason to intervene.
1421971148:
Hvorsuli

Hvorsuli, also known as the "Snow Spires", is a ruined city of thin spires and massive gates located within a glacier in northern Iobaria. Only the foremost gatehouse and a few broken domes lie outside the glacier and allow treasure hunters and explorers access to the ruins. The creators of the city are unknown, but there are mentions of a great power that glows in the heart of the city, contained by the ice god Rheth.
1422379118:
Tymon

The city-state of Tymon lies southwest of the city of Daggermark on the very western borders of the River Kingdoms next to the nation of Razmiran. Tymon is renowned for its gladiators, as it boasts both a huge arena and many prestigious gladiator colleges.
1422433988:
Ghivel's Townhouse

Ghivel's Townhouse is located in Rego Corna and was the home of a family devoted to Abadar who suffered greatly during the chaos of 4710 AR. The townhouse contained statues and paintings of their god, as well as a basement where they hid things they didn't want others to see.
1433105918:
Varnhold

Varnhold is a seemingly abandoned settlement located southwest of Restov, near the northern Tors of Levenies in the Nomen Heights region of the Stolen Lands. It is notable for being inexplicably and suddenly vacated save for a single word scrawled on one of its buildings.
1437101398:
Turtleback Ferry

4 locations:
  • Graul Homestead
  • Pendaka
  • Whitewillow
  • Wreck of the Paradise
1443765688:
Iadara

The illusion-shrouded city of Iadara (pronounced eye-uh-DAR-ah) is the beautiful capital of the elven kingdom of Kyonin.
1454097438:
Witchcaves

The Witchcaves are a series of deep caverns in the south eastern region of Irrisen's Hoarwood province. According to legend, great power lies in these caves, which are also suspected of having an entrance to the Darklands.

In 3937 AR the survivors of the failed Harcatha Rebellion fled to the caves in the hope of recruiting Darklands allies to help their cause. Nothing more was heard of them until 4079 AR, when an insane force of Jadwiga emerged from the caves and rampaged across the region until they were wiped out by Irriseni forces led by General Chernim Karina.

Queen Karina, Irrisen's then ruler, ordered the caves sealed. However, a few adventurers recently defied the ban and investigated the caves—and found them overrun by qlippoth. The leader of these fiends is an augnagar called Ulkrostaz. Some of the Harcartha who entered the caves still live, serving as breeding stock and subject to ongoing torture.
1459449698:
Wolfhead

The Wolfhead inn, located in Andoran's Darkmoon Vale on a crumbling hillside west of the River Foam and the Wolfrun Hills, is so named for its design as a wolf's head with its mouth opening over a 50-foot drop into a patch of wolfsbane. The inn has nine rooms and a boarding capacity of 17.

Built in 4213 AR by the eccentric gnome architect Las Stopbiddle, the inn has been owned and passed down for generations. As of 4708 AR, its owner was the halfling Dopp, who employs guards but welcomes anyone to the inn as patrons.
1459687788:
Qumarin

The remnants of the lost and accursed city of Qumarin are located in the southern Meraz Desert of Qadira. Its location has been largely forgotten except for a few reclusive girtablilu tribes and it is avoided by everyone.
1463451368:
Flameford

Flameford is a small Shoanti camp near Kaer Maga.
1466185848:
Skathen

Skathen is a trade city on the Brokenbridge River in Taldor's Northern Tandak Prefecture.
1477685228:
Dunholme

Dunholme is a ruined settlement in Taldor's Verduran Forest. The site fell victim to a mysterious event known as "the Dunholme Doom" in which an as-yet-unknown threat emerged from a well, first claiming the lives of townsfolk Verniya Klamer, Maix Vivermer, and Jasmique Tunhold. A trade road still runs through Dunholme toward Belhaim and Wispil further north.
1486349368:
Windarium

The Windarium is a clockworks shop in Westgate that is going through difficult times. The shop is located in a shopping district frequented by those looking for parts or repairs. Founded by Symo in 4705 AR, the shop began to gain popularity when he met Mirtion, who had already successfully run several shops. In 4710 AR, they married, and Symo was promoted in 4716 AR. The shop's popularity was booming, and everything was going great. After the Black Echelon Uprising of 4717 AR, Symo was promoted again, and with no time for the shop, Mirtion has had to run it alone. He has no idea about the engineering required to maintain clockworks and, demotivated, has put his passion into the badgers who fill the shop and generate complaints about the bribes they take from customers and employees alike.
1516595748:
Port Peril

1521045088:
New Krane

New Krane is the fortress headquarters of the Hellknight Order of the Coil and is located in Sargava on the shore of Desperation Bay, northeast of the capital city of Eleder.

Magnimar
