1572412218:
Crimson Citadel

The headquarters of the feared Red Mantis assassins, the Crimson Citadel is one of the few settlements on Mediogalti Island aside from the city of Ilizmagorti. Only the Red Mantis know its exact location and features, but there are numerous rumours about the place. This means that anyone who wishes to contact the Red Mantis must make their intentions known in the more cosmopolitan city of Ilizmagorti.
1580791738:
Bloomwreath

Bloomwreath is a town situated in western Kyonin around a greenhouse called the Great Blossom. Botanists recognize the plant species preserved in the greenhouse as species lost during Earthfall.

The village's activities revolve around botany, from growing flowers to cutting topiary. The process is overseen by the founder, known as the High Gardener, who considers it her mission to preserve plants from around the world.
1586136388:
Icerift Castle

During the First Mendevian Crusade, the government of Mendev decided to establish a stronghold on its northern border with the Worldwound. As a result, work began on building Icerift Castle at the source of the West Sellen River near the Crown of the World. Before the castle was finished, however, the Mendevian forces had a change of heart. Conscious of the difficulty in supplying and reinforcing such a remote outpost, they decided to cancel the construction. A party of soldiers was sent north to escort home the construction crew and their guards, only to find that they had all been slaughtered and their hearts had been torn out. Even stranger, the attack did not appear to have been carried out by demons. The superstitious indigenous people of Mendev blamed the legendary wendigo for the killing, but no investigation was carried out; instead the site was declared anathema and removed from the nation's records.
1592405698:
Corpselight

Corpselight is a small farming settlement in the undead nation of Geb situated north of the Axan Wood and about two day's travel east of Graydirge.

Corpselight is not a traditional farming community however. The town raises intelligent humanoids for use as food and sustenance for the various undead beings throughout Geb. These human chattel are kept in pens until they are ready to be shipped out. Standard crops are also grown in order to feed them.
1606541008:
Kravenkus

The dwarven hold of Kravenkus stands in the World's Edge Mountains, in Taldor. It is one of the original Sky Citadels, and still maintains a connection to the Darklands.

Like the other Sky Citadels, Kravenkus was built by the dwarves soon after their Quest for Sky as a fortress from which to continue their epic war against the orcs. Kravenkus is one of the four remaining in dwarven hands.

Kravenkus is something of a ghost town, consisting of around 2,500 dwarves protected by a small garrison of only 400. The inhabitants typically reside in the central "keep" area. Most of the outskirts of the citadel have been abandoned due to regular attacks from Darklands creatures, including hryngars, mongrelfolk, and worse. While a few stubborn souls eke out a living in the empty halls and corridors, most of the remaining population live in the citadel's nearly abandoned central keep. The mines that once provided the wealth of the Kravenkus have long since been played out, resulting in the departure of most of the populace to the Five Kings Mountains.

Despite these hardships, the people of Kravenkus are proud of their home and refuse to seal the Darklands tunnels, viewing them as a connection to their life prior to the Quest for Sky. The dwarves welcome adventurers who offer assistance against the underground menaces.
1617695988:
Nightswallow's Rest

Nightswallow's Rest is a town of humans in southern Jinin, along the Silkstring River near Jinin's border with Songbai.
1670430598:
Zhukuo Tegeg

Zhukuo Tegeg is the communal cemetery of the town of Ngozu on the Mgange Cove island of the Shackles. As of 4712 AR, several juju zombies had recently emerged from the eerie cemetery. Chief wendifa Ajuoga Baas put down many of these undead monsters but sought aid from outsiders to end the zombie menace. Some of Ajuoga's followers thus began to doubt her worthiness as chief wendifa.
1672178218:
Baallalota

Baallalota, the Swamp's Heart, is located in the heart of the Graidmere Swamp north-east of Lake Prophyria, near the far north-eastern border of the Ustalavic county of Sinaria. Baallalota is an area of ground within the Graidmere where daughters of the swamp dance a ritual on the night of the vernal equinox to determine the year's Queen of Baallalota.
1678189368:
Sabkha

Sabkha is a shallow basin filled with gritty salt-laden sand. It is the primary source for the nagri salt that farmers use to make pesh. Farmers travel to Sabkha once a season to sift salt out of the dirt and carry sacks of it home. Merchants flock to Sabkha to collect nagri salt, which they then sell in the markets of Katapesh. Once a month or so, heavy rains fill Sabkha's shallow basin with trapped rainwater. After several days, the water drains out through the rock, leaving new salt deposits on the surface.
1682965728:
Sandpoint

4 locations:
  • Birdcruncher Cave
  • Pyre
  • Raven's Watch
  • The Old Light
1692896598:
Aerie

The Aerie is a fortress of Andoren Eagle Knights located in the northern reaches of the Kortos Mounts on the Isle of Kortos. Knights assigned to the post deal with thin air due to its elevation, poor food, and local hostilities in order to monitor and prevent any potential threats to Andoran that might originate from the Isle.

As of 4720 AR, several scouts of the Aerie had recently been found decapitated. Despite a lack of evidence suggesting a suspect, most of the fort's troops believed the nearby Gorebreather tribe of minotaurs were responsible.
1698144618:
Scriveners' Square

Scriveners' Square is where the offices of most of the city's newspapers are located, publishing articles on politics, fashion, and entertainment for all who buy them. The fountain in the center of the square resembles an inkwell, and one can hear kids shouting about the latest local news, advertising their particular paper, the sound of the printing presses—dozens of them running at once—overpowering their voices.
1699674608:
Eodred's Square

Eodred's Square forms a large plaza at the western end of the High Bridge in the Varisian city of Korvosa. Eodred's Square is popular with both locals and visitors to the city, and is a popular meeting place for both in the Midland district.
1710166878:
Gallery Rousa

Gallery Rousa is a popular and controversial art gallery in the Wise Quarter whose exhibitions change regularly and whose subject matter is criticized for its provocative nature.
1714495288:
Ongongte Overlook

Ongongte Overlook is a narrow bluff in the southwestern corner of Mgange Cove in the Shackles that is considered a holy site to the worshipers of juju.
1717788468:
Sunken City

Sunken City is a Thassilonian ruin under the waves in the Howling Straits in the northern reaches of Broken Bay. Known as Udeomel to historians, the sunken city would have been a northerly settlement of Cyrusian.
1723206398:
Tenna's

Citadel Crest ward, in the Heights District, is the only ward in Korvosa having no inns by law. People requiring overnight stays, instead, have the choice from three bed and breakfast establishments, all of which are owned by nobles. In order of price, they are:

  • the Frisky Unicorn, the cheapest;
  • Upslope House; and
  • Tenna's, the most expensive.

In order of size, Tenna's is the smallest and Upslope House the largest.

Tenna's has four luxurious rooms able to comfortably house 11 guests, providing more pampering than anywhere else in Korvosa. The bed and breakfast is situated on Volshyenek Loop opposite the estate of the powerful House Ornelos.
1730026868:
Westcrown

  • Korradath

  • Taranik House

  • Thesing's Tower
  • Cader Boneyard

  • and 49 others…
1748343488:
Skelt

A place that is nigh impregnable to its enemies, the city of Skelt is of strategic importance to the whole of Nirmathas, being situated along the Shining River, which flows into the Tourondel River in northern Nirmathas.
1751504628:
Deepmar Penal Colony

Under Chelaxian rule, Deepmar hosted a small penal colony known as Deepmar Penal Colony, where the prisoners mined crystals useful as arcane reagents. The prison camps were abandoned before Ravounel's secession in the Kintargan Rebellion, and the new government has let them remain so.
1752090098:
Monastery of Tala

Located in central Mendev, the Monastery of Tala caters to many faiths of the crusaders going to fight the demons in the Worldwound. Run by a cloistered order of monks from numerous faiths named the Brotherhood of Tala, it was founded in 4702 AR as a place where the various churches could conduct research on how best to combat the demonic forces of the Worldwound.
1757601438:
Candlestone Caverns

The Candlestone Caverns are a vast labyrinth of living limestone tunnels and caves located in the northeastern Aspodell Mountains of Andoran near the border with Cheliax. They were exposed only two centuries ago in an earthquake. Deep below the Candlestone Caverns is the Endless Gulf, a rift in Golarion's crust that connects to the Darklands realms of Nar-Voth, Sekamina, and Orv.
1758223348:
Tolguth

Like the Realm of the Mammoth Lords' largest settlement, Icestair, Tolguth is nestled against the icy walls of the Crown of the World. Unlike Icestair it is a warm, almost tropical town, surrounded by great ferns, rhododendrons, and green cycads. The reason for this vast difference in temperature is the volcanic vents and thermal springs that dot the area. Within this valley roam fantastic creatures that are not found anywhere else in Avistan. These include duck-billed dinosaurs, swift raptors, mighty tyrannosaurs and predatory terror birds like the axe beak. Unsurprisingly, the city is walled for the protection of its inhabitants, but these walls provide only a slight hindrance to the largest of the valley's creatures. Despite being surrounded by a dinosaur-infested wilderness, the biggest danger to the people of Tolguth comes from the Worldwound and its fell demonic influence.
1762248418:
Corveilles

Corveilles, sometimes spelled Corveiles, was a large city in the Thassilonian kingdom of Cyrusian. As of 4707 AR, its location had not been rediscovered.
1765790218:
Gembasket

The Gembasket spans an area in the Barrier Wall mountains approximately a mile in diameter, across razored peaks, unexpected drops, rocky ground, and impassable rises. Natural resources are abundant within the Gembasket, including nodes of gemstones, precious metals, and veins of minerals that run its rocky walls. A narrow and dangerous path navigable only by experienced travelers winds up through the mountains to the Gembasket.
1777388478:
Arwyll Stead

Arwyll Stead is a mining town in the far north of the Gravelands, on the border with the dangerous orc territory of the Hold of Belkzen and close to the haunted county of Virlych in Ustalav. Residents of the town are commonly referred to as "Steaders".

The town is located on the Path Road that led from Vigil into Ustalav.
1786784978:
Revousa Ditches

The Katharevousa River meets the Yolubilis River in the Revousa Ditches, deep rents in the ground that are prone to flooding. The site is so well traveled by riverboats that temporary shops set up on anchored, floating platforms at all times except during the rainy season. The Ditches are believed to be home to a rusalka.
1810438298:
Xin-Bakrakhan

Xin-Bakrakhan was the capital of the ancient Thassilonian Realm of Wrath, Bakrakhan. Most of it was destroyed during Earthfall and sank beneath the waters of the Varisian Gulf. What little remains intact can be found on Rivenrake Island, surrounding the honeycombed massif called Hollow Mountain.
1838944978:
Tomb of the Living

5 locations:
  • District Courthouse
  • Guild of Wonders
  • Mithril Hall
  • To Eat the World
  • Westgate Bathhouse
1843705398:
Zhonar

Zhonar is a volcano located in the eastern Zho Mountains of Qadira. The eruptions of Zhonar and its companion Zhobl covered the nearby town of Shadun in ash and dust, forcing its inhabitants to abandon it.
1854784328:
Penitents

The Penitents are a group of 18 religious pilgrims who as of 4712 AR had been stranded on the island of Hesmene's Grotto in the Shackles. They are led by the paladin Jayhalle Messierre, and include worshipers of both Erastil and Abadar. Messierre does his best to keep the arguments and petulant bickering between the members of the Penitents to a minimum, all the while fending off the unusual and deadly creatures found on the island. They occasionally come into conflict with the Merry Pranksters, a group of pirates who are also stranded on the island, and even more rarely join forces with the buccaneers to fight against their common foe, the rusalka Blue Hesmene.
1877505438:
Sholay

Sholay is a huge pool of magma in the lower southern foothills of the Lohaparbat Mountains in the Falling Mountains region of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. The mechanical god Dhuangir is said to lie buried beneath this stretch of mountains, and Sholay is the result of his hryngar followers' attempt to excavate parts of Dhuangir from beneath the range.
1884395248:
Iqaliat

Iqaliat is a small village of Erutaki humans in the Crown of the World, built into the side of Alasek Ridge on the southern boundary of the High Ice some miles west of the Path of Aganhei. It is constructed beneath the cliff's overhanging ridge. Most of the town is made up of small stone and clay brick buildings and subsists on water from an underground stream.

Iqaliat is protected by its location beneath the cliffs. Travelers can only enter the village through a gatehouse above several earthen ramps. Other sites in the village include low towers inhabited by the village's chieftain Nalvanaq and its shaman Tunuak, Hearthmistress Sonavut's small house, a goat pen, a kennel for the village's dogs, and a subterranean cistern. A small tunnel leads up to the cliff top, where one can find several inukshuks, altars to the Erutaki wind spirits.

The white dragon Vegsundvaag keeps her lair not far from Iqaliat.
1885613678:
Killik-Tura Oasis

The Killik-Tura Oasis, sometimes spelt Kilik-Tura Oasis, is located in the western reaches of the Footprints of Rovagug region of Osirion, east of the Junira River. The water at the small oasis is cool and plentiful. Killik-Tura Oasis is a popular stop for caravans despite the rumors that large worm-like creatures occasionally emerge from the oasis. The oasis is formed as a perfect circle, effectively bottomless, and its subterranean, water-filled tunnels branch out for miles. A fantastical tale says that the tunnels open into dry caverns filled with crawling horrors and feral humanoids. The truth is that Crawlbrother, a distinguished agent of the nearby Darklands ghoulish city of Nemret-Noktoria, lurks beneath the waters.
1887442778:
Tripolne

Tripolne is the seat of the Backar territory of Molthune.
1902238518:
Elfmeet

The Elfmeet is a location in Icemark where the Ilverani set up camp whenever they visit the region. They generally travel along a coastal route to avoid the dangers of the inner Stormspear Mountains. Elfmeet features a double ring of standing stones overlooking the sea. The Varki do not treat this site as one of their villages but often visit the location and engage in trade with Ilverani. The Ilverani are fond of Varki scrimshaw, and often trade potent magical items for well-crafted Varki art.
1908898238:
Sanmeshul

Sanmeshul is a Qadiran city that lies to the west of the centre of the Maharev region along the Meraz River. This region is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira and Sanmeshul is the second largest city of the region, behind only Qaharid in terms of size.
1916156388:
Varook's Deep

Varook's Deep is both the lowest dwarf-inhabited point of the Five Kings Mountains at 17,621 feet below sea level, a defensive city that keeps watch over the Darklands, and a trading post that hosts caligni, drathnelars, and underground fey. Its garrison is led by General Flinda Strikespark.

Due to the city's depth, few surface-dwelling dwarves visit Varook's Deep.
1926491268:
Absalom Academy of Law

The Absalom Academy of Law is one of its most influential educational institutions outside the nation, attracting students from as far away as Tian Xia who travel there to learn the city's laws and how to manage a bureaucratic body as large as Absalom's.
1944258938:
Kamawgyar Shrine

The abandoned Kamawgyar Shrine is located in the Zho Mountains of Qadira northeast of the Pashman River. It features a massive statue of the god Irori placed over a natural cave where the priests who once served the shrine lived. It is now the home of a mother and daughter harpy who were exiled from the large harpy community at Al-Bashir, along with a small retinue of dire corbies.
1945012488:
Highforest

Highforest is a petrified forest of unknown origin in the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas. It is speculated that the cedar trees that made up the original forest were fossilized by magical ash from Earthfall. Aberrations and vermin have made the forest home.
1992637008:
Ghrinitshahara

Ghrinitshahara is the capital of the Rampore Isles in the Shackles. The busy town is located on the central island of the archipelago, and as of 4712 AR it was famous for its large harbor and shipyards, where captured ships are repaired and refitted for sale to buyers across the Shackles, Sargava (now known as Vidrian), and the Inner Sea region.

As of 4712 AR, the cruel rakshasa Bedu Hanji ruled the Rampore Isles from his sprawling palace in Ghrinitshahara. The town once resembled the hamlets of the distant Kingdom of Vudra, home to the Vudrani mariners who established the settlement. When Bedu Hanji infiltrated Rampore's loose organization and became the master of the isles in 4662 AR, Ghrinitshahara became the center of a thriving business around the trade of humanoid slaves.
1994310918:
Cathedral of Abadar

4 locations:
  • Deverin Villa
  • Fish Tank
  • House of Welcome
  • Kaijitsu Townhouse
2002885488:
Shadowreach

Despite its appearance as a rural paradise, locals have shunned the ivy-covered hunting lodge named Shadowreach in Nidal's southern Uskwood for years. The area is infested with lethal shadow creatures, and rumors circulate about even more perilous dangers lurking among the nearby trees.
2005338548:
Sylirica

The Iblydan city of Sylirica is the capital city of the city-state of Pol-Sylirica.
2028082578:
Kublan Grove

The Kublan Grove is no clump of woodland but a tangled mass of rafters, scaffolds, and masonry that serves as a testament to the flighty whims of the Taldan aristocracy. Located in Taldor's Lingian Prefecture, the Kublan Grove began life as a construction project that started soon after the prefecture's rise in popularity. Approximately twenty years ago, three Opparan baronets, all cousins from House Kublan, borrowed against their family's ancestral lands, buying up several miles of farmland to construct a grand retreat for the nobility. In their lust to provide every possible luxury, the project's scope increased massively, bloating from an original plan for a simple manor house to, eventually, being an entire community devoted to vice and hedonism, but it was not to be.

Funding dried up and construction halted. Now the half-finished buildings lie abandoned amidst a forest of scaffolding, empty save for a few wild animals and a community of squatters. House Kublan occasionally hires bands of thugs to sweep through the grove and chase off the squatters, desperately hoping to attract new investors as the Lingian Prefecture's popularity grows.
2029309528:
Grand Grounds of the Splendid Statuary

The Grand Grounds of the Splendid Statuary is a market and showcase of statuary near Rhuzam, Druma, that is owned and operated by the dwarven Prophet Jurgan Khrest. Khrest has long employed the finest sculptors from across the Inner Sea region to craft the mundane and magical works he displays on the grounds, though his expenses have begun to overtake his profits and he has begun resorting to using his sold statues to scry secrets from his fellow prophets.
2040525598:
Storasta

Storasta was once one of the greatest cities of the Kellid nation of Sarkoris in northeastern Avistan. Founded in 3844 AR, it was finally abandoned in 4644 AR like the rest of the Worldwound. During its occupation, Storasta was overrun by demons until it was freed at the end of the Fifth Mendevian Crusade. Due to its complete corruption, however, the crusaders decided to raze the city rather than rebuild it.
2041386178:
Krihirik village

The Krihirik village, located on the eastern edge of the Kaava Lands, is home to the Krihirik tribe of tripkees. The tribe has been in conflict with the lizardfolk of the region for years, losing more and more territory. In 4715 AR, members of the Aspis Consortium attacked the village in search of a fragment of the Sky Key, but agents of the Pathfinder Society arrived in time to stop them.
2042000748:
Avendale

Avendale is the capital city of the River Kingdom of Touvette.
2057082098:
Groggy Froggy

The Groggy Froggy is a Westgate tavern that changed hands when its previous owner was petrified by a gorgon in 4714 AR, and the tavern was passed on to Annara Laskin and the other staff members. The statue of the cruel tavern keeper stands in the middle of the tavern with its mouth agape in a ridiculed expression of terror, and the tavern's popularity has reached unprecedented levels. A pastime was created where customers could paint on the statue, giving it a new look, and it was magically cleaned every week. This pastime stopped when the statue mysteriously disappeared one day, and there are conflicting accounts, with some saying that an enemy destroyed the statue while others say that an ally unpetrified it, although it's hard to believe he had any.
2057824568:
Port Peril

9 locations:
  • Colvaas Gibbet
  • F & M's Exotic Meats
  • Fort Hazard
  • Harborhorn
  • Latchmin's Folly
  • Mermaid's Bucket
  • Mystic's Redoubt
  • Sea Wrack Chapel
  • Siren's Lash
2067768808:
Whiterush

Whiterush is a small, isolated village located in the Verduran Forest region of Andoran, along a patch of rapids on the Sellen River. Its inhabitants are known for their friendly, chatty nature, and the town itself boasts cheery-looking houses covered in thatched roofing. The inhabitants of Whiterush are renowned for their distrust of the residents of nearby Rippleden, claiming that Whiterush and its residents are obviously the superior of the two rival villages.
2080107458:
Thesing's Tower

Thesing's Tower was the residence of Thesing Umbero Ulvauno, the popular but arrogant actor of Westcrown, who, after his death, rose as a vampire seeking revenge against the actors who stole his thunder in his last performance. The stone tower served as a guard post in the past and is in good condition, standing up to 30 feet tall.
2082704268:
Firepelt

The Firepelt is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. There are no known entrances into this piling, which stands 160 feet tall.
2086150658:
Turtleback Ferry

The town of Turtleback Ferry was until recently a relatively quiet frontier hamlet located on the banks of the Skull River in central Varisia. Events related to the failed giant invasion of the Varisian lowlands in 4707 AR caused quite a bit of disturbance, but the town has since regained much of its former innocuousness.
2102231148:
Skala Foothills

The Skala Foothills, in the southern part of the Irriseni province of Wintercrux lie in the shadow of the Kodar Mountains and are rich in resources. More than two dozen mining camps provide Irrisen with tin, copper, iron and gold. Moreover rock quarries produce limestone and granite. According to some mining experts, gems may well be found in the future. A large network of shipping trails cross the Skala Foothills connecting the mining camps to Hope Lost and the town of Zlatomesto.

Many sasquatch families inhabit the wooded areas of the Skala Foothills. Also brutish giants, known as ved, launch small raids against the mining camps and quarries.
2104605528:
Shevaroth

The ruins of the city of Shevaroth, once one of Kyonin's most populous pre-Earthfall cities, are located on the plains southeast of the Fierani Forest. Abandoned by the elves fleeing Earthfall to Castrovel, Shevaroth fell under the dominion of the nascent demon lord Treerazer in 2633 AR, and as of 4725 AR it had not been retaken.
2120306028:
Red Kestrel Imports

Red Kestrel Imports had existed in Magnimar since 4702 AR. Its red warehouse in the northern portion Ordellia district flies the flags of the six nations it operates out of—Absalom, Cheliax, Osirion, Katapesh, Sargava, and, of course, Magnimar.

As of 4712 AR, it was run by Anton Ghanafini.
2129067978:
Silvertree

Silvertree is a small settlement in northwestern Vidrian. Under the rule of Sargava, it was known as Crown's End.
2138195018:
Thanesrest Mausoleum

Thanesrest Mausoleum, on the island of Orthost in the Linnorm Kingdom of Broken Bay, is a Thassilonian building that was turned into a mausoleum for kings of Broken Bay. This practice was halted after the body of King Kernic Quicktongue rose up during his funeral, after being possessed of a demon, to murder then create undead of all the mourners.

It is the greatest mausoleum on the isle and was not originally built to inter the dead. The elaborate mausoleum may have originally served as a Thassilonian castle or prison. Its chambers below extend for many miles deep under Orthost.
